criteria.criterion: MCP protocol facts must be grounded in official MCP documentation.
Side-effecting tools and sensitive resources require explicit user consent controls.
Tool arguments and tool results must be validated before use.
Authorization and roots must use least privilege and avoid token passthrough.
Tool use, resource access and consent decisions must be auditable without logging secrets.
Consent and permission controls must be represented as invocation-gating states, not as blanket tool-disabled states.
Tool invocations must be classified into tier-1, tier-2 or tier-3 with gating behaviour aligned to the selected tier.
